examples.title: 2-to-1 Multiplexer
examples.code:
FUNCTION MUX_2to1(I0, I1, S)
BEGIN
    IF S = 0 THEN
        RETURN I0
    ELSE
        RETURN I1
    END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: The function takes two data inputs I0, I1 and select line S. Returns I0 when S=0 and I1 when S=1.

examples.title: 4-to-1 Multiplexer in Python
examples.description: Python implementation of 4:1 MUX using list indexing
examples.code:
def mux_4to1(inputs, select):
    """
    inputs: list of 4 values [I0, I1, I2, I3]
    select: 2-bit binary as integer 0-3
    Returns selected input
    """
    if select < 0 or select > 3:
        raise ValueError("Select must be 0-3")
    return inputs[select]

# Example usage
data_inputs = [0, 1, 1, 0]  # I0=0, I1=1, I2=1, I3=0
select_line = 2  # Binary 10 selects I2
output = mux_4to1(data_inputs, select_line)
print(f"Output: {output}")  # Output: 1
examples.explanation: The select value acts as index to directly select from inputs array. Very efficient for hardware description.

examples.title: General N-to-1 MUX in C
examples.description: Generalized MUX implementation for any power of 2 inputs
examples.explanation: General implementation that can handle any 2^n inputs. Converts binary select lines to integer index.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of select lines

examples.title: 4-to-1 MUX in VHDL
examples.description: VHDL structural description of 4:1 multiplexer for FPGA implementation
examples.explanation: VHDL hardware description for synthesis. Uses case statement which maps directly to multiplexer selection logic in FPGA/CPLD.
